params._roi_note: start_x/start_y centre the 248x248 ROI on the beam parked at coarse bias 2/2 (fine 1.5/2.15): probe-verified 2026-07-21 after realignment, full-sensor CoM (x 919, y 507). Re-derive with contrib/probe_frame.json only if the alignment (not the bias) changes -- the ROI now follows the beam by cropping instead of re-biasing.

pipeline._comment: Send raw frame to watch_steering.jl on port 64730
pipeline._note: decimation must MATCH the 64731 sink below, so the frame and the CoM cross drawn together come from the same iteration. 64 gives ~36 Hz at a 2310 Hz loop -- about what a GR heatmap can redraw.

pipeline._comment: Track a 15x15 window centred on the previous CoM, so the stray reflection never enters the sum.
pipeline._note: Output stays normalized across the whole 64x64 image, NOT the window: the setpoint is still frame centre and the PID gains below are unchanged. With the beam-centred ROI the stray reflection sits INSIDE the frame at ~(10, 28) and carries flux comparable to the beam, so the old whole-frame acquisition phase can settle between the two spots -- init the window on the beam (frame centre) explicitly instead. The reflection is 22 px from the beam, well outside the 15 px window.

pipeline._comment: Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_steering.jl on port 64731
pipeline._note: keep decimation equal to the 64730 sink above

pipeline._comment: Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_error_ts.jl on port 64732
pipeline._note: NOT decimated: this is a time series, and dropping samples without an anti-alias filter folds high-frequency error back down into the band you're trying to read. The packets are ~48 bytes, so full rate is ~110 kB/s -- cheap.

adaptive predictor (see doc/devices/kalman_filter.md): replaces the error with its own prediction ~2.1 ms (5 samples) ahead, so the pid acts on where the beam will be when the command lands. A/B-validated 2026-07-10 (x 0.91 -> 0.59 px with the pid line oscillators); re-A/B'd 2026-07-13 over 500 s (x closed 0.384 px vs 0.502 px without). KNOWN COSTS at gain=1.0: 0-5 Hz attenuation -8 dB instead of the -24 dB the loop achieves without the kalman, and a ~-0.11 px x DC pointing bias. DO NOT try to fix that with gain<1: run 4 (gain 0.7 + horizon 6, 2026-07-13) collapsed the x loop (sensitivity crossover 28->15 Hz, closed/open 0.94 vs 0.60) while a bode at the same bias showed |P|x unchanged -- the NLMS trains on the blended closed-loop signal and lands in a bad equilibrium. gainy history: prediction on y PUMPED its 30.8 Hz line 3x back when y crossed at ~62 Hz (iy=1300); re-enabled at 1.0 on 2026-07-14 now that fc_y is 24 Hz and y's dominant residual is the predictable 10-30 Hz cluster -- if a y line pumps >2x, set gainy back to 0 (never a fraction). Trains continuously; hands over along a 10 s ramp after start_delay.

params._note2: order 80->1024 (2026-07-15, only change vs attenuation10): 1024 = fs/2.256 spans one beat period of the 2.256 Hz-spaced 19-26 Hz comb that dominates the x residual; order 80 (~29 Hz resolution) could not represent it. horizon 5 = ceil(loop delay 3.93 fr + exposure smear 0.5 fr + DAC ZOH 0.65 fr). Tracking time now 14.8 s (order/(mu*fs)); rollback = order 80. See attenuation_test.json.

pipeline._note: start_delay must be >= the piplate start_delay below. Holding the fine DAC channels without also holding the pid is useless: the integrator keeps winding on the large startup error behind the parked output, and the fine channel is at its rail the instant the hold expires. Base params are the x axis (element 1), *y the y axis (element 0). i sets the crossover: f_c = i*|P|/2pi. Lead-compensated design 2026-07-14 evening (plant from the fine bodes @ bias 2.52/1.62: Kx=0.427, Ky=0.300, tau_loop=1.81 ms; grid search over the attenuation9 open PSD with PM>=45, Ms<=2.0): C(s) = i/s * (s+2pi*8)/(s+2pi*32) on both axes -- the lead's +37 deg buys fc 46->64 Hz (x) / 24->65 Hz (y) at PM 67, paid with 4x less gain below 8 Hz (affordable: 0-10 Hz closed was 0.05 px). Oscillator phases are plant+sensitivity WITH the lead in L: phi = -360*f*tau + angle(1/(1+L)); RECOMPUTE if i/iy/lead/bias change (attenuation8 lesson, validated by attenuation9: x 0.400->0.282 px). Line centers on multi-run measured positions (wander ~0.5 Hz/run); y 11.3 oscillator added (0.095 px line, steady across runs). Rollback = attenuation9 tune: i=680/iy=500, no lead, see attenuation_test.json _note4. Full rationale there.

params._note1: pid is NOT in coarse mode, so its output is plain [y, x]: ch0 (y fine, +2 V/unit) takes index 0, ch1 (x fine, -2 V/unit) index 1 -- verified by step test 2026-07-10 (ch0 -> CoM y, ch1 -> CoM x, positive i = negative feedback on both). Coarse scales are 0 (parked at bias) per the push-test rule: they had been left live at +-2 with unverified sign, which risks positive feedback; with the beam-centred ROI the fine stages have full authority and the coarse stages are not needed. The coarse biases (0.8, 2.394 V) are the ones the ROI position was derived under.
params._note2: baud 460800 is about pacing, not the wire: the port is USB CDC (baud is cosmetic) but the bridge throttles itself to ~440 cmds/s at the default 115200 -- at a 2310 Hz loop that is a ~2.3 ms command ZOH stacked on the 1.4 ms path delay. Measured 2026-07-10: the bridge drains ~1800 cmds/s; pacing at 460800 (~1770/s) keeps latency flat, while 921600 overruns the drain rate and bufferbloats ~10 ms into a full FIFO. Do not raise past 460800.

params._note: log the filtered command stream; move this before the biquad to capture the raw error for an FFT
